source_articles.title: 2026 Multi-country Hantavirus Cluster Linked to Cruise Ship
Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel - Multi-country (Update, 13 May 2026)
What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak

notes: 70-year-old Dutch man, first identified case in the outbreak; fell ill on board MV Hondius with fever, headache, abdominal pain, diarrhea; died on board April 11, 2026. Body taken ashore at Saint Helena April 24. No samples were taken from him at the time (hantavirus was not yet suspected), which is why WHO classifies his death as PROBABLE rather than confirmed.

source_articles.title: 2026 Multi-country Hantavirus Cluster Linked to Cruise Ship
Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel - Multi-country (Update, 13 May 2026)

notes: Dutch wife of case-001. Disembarked at Saint Helena April 24; died in hospital in Johannesburg, South Africa, April 26. Lab-tested before death; classified as confirmed Andes virus death by WHO.

source_articles.title: American cruise ship passenger tests positive for hantavirus after evacuation (initial report; later cleared)
What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel - Multi-country (Update, 13 May 2026)
U.S. cruise passengers arrive in the U.S. after one tests positive for hantavirus
2026 Multi-country Hantavirus Cluster Linked to Cruise Ship

notes: American MV Hondius passenger admitted to UNMC's biocontainment unit, Nebraska — identified as Dr. Stephen Kornfeld. Initially reported as mildly PCR-positive on board the ship (NPR, May 10). Follow-up testing in the US was negative; per WHO DON601 (May 13), results were declared inconclusive. Per PBS / CBS News (May 13–15), Dr. Kornfeld tested negative on the second lab run and was cleared from biocontainment, moved to the general quarantine unit. WHO has REMOVED him from the official case tally; CDC confirms no current confirmed US cases. Retained in our tracker with case_type=confirmed (frozen at original classification per methodology) but current_status='excluded' reflects the medical clearance.

source_articles.title: Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel - Multi-country

notes: Man who disembarked MV Hondius at Tristan da Cunha (UK Overseas Territory) on April 14; symptoms began April 28; classified as probable case; in stable condition in isolation.

source_articles.title: Hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel - Multi-country (Update, 13 May 2026)
Andes hantavirus outbreak in cruise ship, 12 May 2026

notes: AGGREGATE — 3 additional confirmed cases per WHO 2026-DON601 (May 13, 2026; total 11 cases globally: 8 confirmed + 2 probable + 1 inconclusive, including 3 deaths). 2 cases previously in this aggregate have now been split out as France and Spain. Remaining 3 individual cases not yet publicly identified in trusted-source reporting; will be split out as country-specific confirmations land.

Live updates: Passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship being monitored at US medical units

notes: 15 American former MV Hondius passengers in National Quarantine Unit at UNMC; asymptomatic, under contact monitoring. NOT counted in WHO/ECDC headline case count.

source_articles.title: Live updates: Passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship being monitored at US medical units
Hantavirus live updates: Atlanta passengers relocate to Nebraska quarantine facility

notes: Two former MV Hondius passengers; originally transferred to Emory University in Atlanta, Georgia. Per ABC News (May 16) and updated WHO/CDC reports, both individuals were relocated to UNMC's quarantine unit in Omaha, Nebraska to consolidate all 18 American passengers at a single facility. Asymptomatic; monitoring continues at UNMC.

source_articles.title: Hantavirus live updates: Atlanta passengers relocate to Nebraska quarantine facility
2026 Multi-country Hantavirus Cluster Linked to Cruise Ship

notes: AGGREGATE — 21 additional contacts under public-health monitoring across 13 US states whose specific locations have not been publicly identified. Per CDC (May 14, 2026), 41 people across 16 US states are being monitored following potential Andes virus exposure from the MV Hondius cruise. Our named-location case rows (NE × 16, GA × 2, NJ × 2, UK dual-national × 1 at UNMC) account for 20 of those; this aggregate row covers the remaining 21 distributed across 13 unnamed states. Will be split out as specific-state reporting becomes available.

source_articles.title: Hantavirus live updates: Canadian passenger tests positive for Andes virus
Andes hantavirus outbreak in cruise ship, 17 May 2026

notes: Canadian former MV Hondius passenger; one of four Canadians repatriated. Tested positive for Andes virus on May 15, 2026, per Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC). Hospitalized in isolation along with their partner (see case-016, partner tested negative so far). Specific Canadian city/province not publicly identified in current reporting; will be enriched as PHAC publishes details.

source_articles.title: Hantavirus live updates: Canadian passenger tests positive for Andes virus

notes: Partner of case-015 (the Canadian confirmed-positive passenger); also a former MV Hondius passenger. Hospitalized in isolation as a precaution; tested negative for Andes virus so far per PHAC. Continued public-health monitoring during the 42-day quarantine window.
